description: Tempdoc 565 §12.6 — the run-rendering single-authority register. The agent run is composed by ONE ordered projection rendered through ONE tool-call primitive; this register declares those canonical authorities and the files allowed to consume them, so a SECOND tool renderer or a SECOND run-assembly structure (the 548 two-authorities class at the render tier — the very fork §12 collapsed) is caught early. Honest scope (§12.10): the early-warning catches a fork that IMPORTS the canonical types / mounts the canonical tag; a re-model that hand-rolls its own tool-card markup or its own run assembly WITHOUT importing them is import-invisible and slips the grep — hence register + DISCIPLINE, not a hard gate (the same ceiling as check-controls-a11y / the §3.B status-tone gate).

toolRenderer.rationale: ONE tool-call render primitive — the compact row (icon · verb+target · risk · status, expandable). The record half and the live half both render through it (§12.3.B); the static `tool · status` label fork was retired in Phase 1.

runProjection.rationale: ONE ordered run projection — the record (projectUnifiedThread) and the live SSE run (projectLiveAgentActivity) project into the SAME UnifiedTurnItem contract, merged and rendered through one item renderer (§12.3.A). No second run-assembly structure.

runSegments.rationale: Tempdoc 565 §26.A — ONE run-segmentation authority. The run's STRUCTURE (workflow node grouping; background-run provenance) is computed ONCE by `assignRunSegments` in the run-projection module, stamped onto the typed `RunSegmentRef` field of `UnifiedTurnItem`, and read by the body (segment → collapsible group) + the spine (segment start → boundary landmark). Before §26 the node structure was dropped into the untyped `attributes` escape hatch and no-op'd (§25.1, the §15.C flatten). A SECOND segmentation pass (another file computing `assignRunSegments`, or re-deriving node grouping) is the §26 leaf-fork — caught here import-visibly. Honest scope (§12.10): a hand-rolled re-segmentation that imports nothing still slips; the typed `RunSegmentRef` seam (only `assignRunSegments` stamps it) is the stronger guard, per §15.D.1 'prefer the seam over the grep gate'.

answerRenderer.rationale: Tempdoc 565 §15.B — ONE answer-text renderer + ONE inline-citation weave. Every mode (agent / RAG / chat / extract / navigate / summarize) renders the answer through `jf-markdown-block` (markdown or `plain` format); the per-sentence `[n]` weave + grounding coloring lives once in its private `decorateCitations`. The RAG flat-text fork (`StreamingTextBlock` + its `cite-ref-click` event) was retired. The claim→`Citation` mapping (the one resolver every RAG site shares) lives in `citationResolve.claimsToCitations`. A SECOND answer-text block tag or a SECOND `claimsToCitations` owner is the §15.A/§15.B leaf-fork class — caught here (same import-visible ceiling as the other two authorities, §12.10).

groundingSemantics.rationale: Tempdoc 565 §15.A/§15.D.1/§6 — ONE grounding-semantics authority. The score→tier→grounding-class/label mapping (the answer↔source similarity classification) lives ONLY in evidenceProjection.ts; the tier is a BRANDED GroundingTier (§15.D.1 typed seam) only `evidenceTier` can mint, and the grounding presentations (`groundingClass`/`groundingLabel`) + the grouping/CSS vocabulary (`tierGroup`/`tierClass`) derive from it. Before §15 this mapping was forked across FOUR sites with TWO threshold sets (MarkdownBlock/StreamingTextBlock `groundingStatus` 0.5/0.2; CitationHoverCard `scoreLabel` 0.5/0.2; this file 0.7/0.3) so one similarity classified DIFFERENTLY per surface. This realizes the §6 'evidence-surface gate' SCORER facet via two checks: (i) only registered consumer sites import the tier-classification symbols (a new importer = a new grounding classifier, forces review); (ii) NO file but the authority re-derives a grounding class from a numeric threshold (a `score >= 0.X ? 'grounded'` shape — the import-INVISIBLE fork the seam alone can't catch). Honest scope (§12.10): import-visible + literal-visible; a fully-obfuscated re-derivation (no recognizable threshold literal, no import) still slips — the same ceiling as the other run-renderer checks.

stepPresentation.rationale: Tempdoc 565 §17 — ONE run-step presentation authority. A run step's { tone, glyph, label, prominence, liveness } is projected ONCE by `runStepPresentation.stepPresentation` (a BRANDED StepPresentation) and rendered by the ONE `<jf-run-node>` primitive; the status→tone and status→glyph facets are SIBLINGS living ONLY in `statusTone.ts`. Before §17 the tone was projected (statusTone) but the glyph/label/prominence/bundle were hand-authored at ≥5 step-render sites (renderRunSpine / renderTraceNode / ToolCallCard / RetrospectivePanel) — tempdoc 559's 'kernel half-applied to presentation' at the run-step element (the identical `statusAccent(status)` line was byte-copied at renderRunSpine:1511 / renderTraceNode:2026). Three checks: (i) only registered toneSites import the status authority symbols — a NEW importer is a second status classifier, forces review; (ii) only registered nodeSites mount `<jf-run-node>`; (iii) NO file but the run-node primitive hand-rolls a status GLYPH char (✓/✕/⊘) as a ternary branch — the import-INVISIBLE glyph fork the import-scope can't catch. Honest scope (§12.10): import + tag + glyph-literal visible; a fully-obfuscated status presentation still slips — the same ceiling as the other run-renderer checks.

toolLineage.rationale: Tempdoc 577 §2.14 Root III (#18) — ONE tool-output text-provenance authority. A tool result's lineage (corpus-quoted vs runtime vs agent-authored) is STAMPED authoritatively by the backend (OutputLineage) into structuredData.lineage and projected ONCE by `toolOutputLineage` / framed by `lineageFrameLabel`; the FE never re-derives the classification from the output text. This is the display half of prompt-injection safety — the answer-frame's mirror for tool spans: a corpus-quoted excerpt is framed as quoted so citation/instruction-shaped text inside it cannot read as the agent's own claim. A SECOND file importing the lineage symbols (a second framing classifier) forces review. Honest scope (§12.10): import-visible only; a hand-rolled re-derivation off structuredData.lineage that imports nothing still slips — register + discipline, same ceiling as groundingSemantics.

searchResultRendering.rationale: Tempdoc 577 Goal 3 §3.9a — ONE search-result evidence rendering shared by the standalone SearchSurface and the unified window's retrieve tier (UnifiedChatView). The per-hit 'Why this result?' disclosure (chips from the per-hit trace + the 'Explain in words' → core.summarize action) lives once in whyThisResult.ts; the facet-chip render (projected from the response's emitted counts + selections) lives once in facetChips.ts. Tempdoc 602 R3 — the per-hit path/snippet PRESENTATION (middle-ellipsis path + query-term `<mark>` highlight) lives once in resultRowPresentation.ts (sibling of 597 R-1's matchCountLabel.ts); before R3 the retrieve tier showed the raw path with no highlighting while the Search surface formatted + highlighted, so the SAME result rendered two ways. Before §3.9a the in-window tier was a leaner FORK that lacked both; folding them into the one window risked re-implementing the same chips a second time. A THIRD file importing these render symbols (a new fork of the why/facet/highlight render) is caught here — register a consumer only with review. Honest scope (§12.10): import-visible only; a hand-rolled re-implementation that imports nothing still slips — register + discipline, the same ceiling as groundingSemantics / toolLineage.
